---
title: "Codebook: Cleaned Data"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
jupyter: data-pipeline-venv
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path

CLEANED_DIR = Path("../cleaned")

# SPF cleaned tables: forecast_individual (wide), forecaster_survey
TABLES = {
    "forecast_individual": {
        "file": "forecast_individual.csv",
        "description": "Individual (microdata) forecasts in wide form: one row per (survey_year, survey_quarter, forecaster_id) with one column per forecast horizon.",
        "primary_key": ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
        "foreign_keys": {},
        "variables": {
            "survey_year": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey year (BEA/NIPA definition)."},
            "survey_quarter": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey quarter (1–4)."},
            "forecaster_id": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "SPF forecaster identifier."},
        }
    },
    "forecaster_survey": {
        "file": "forecaster_survey.csv",
        "description": "One row per (survey_year, survey_quarter, forecaster_id) with industry.",
        "primary_key": ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
        "foreign_keys": {},
        "variables": {
            "survey_year": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey year."},
            "survey_quarter": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey quarter (1–4)."},
            "forecaster_id": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "SPF forecaster identifier."},
            "industry": {"type": "string", "unit": "-", "desc": "Forecaster industry code."},
        }
    },
    "adjusted_cpi10": {
        "file": "adjusted_cpi10.csv",
        "description": "Adjusted 10-year CPI expectations: one row per (survey_year, survey_quarter, forecaster_id) with adjusted_cpi10 only, after removing realized CPI1 terms and re-averaging over the remaining quarters.",
        "primary_key": ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
        "foreign_keys": {},
        "variables": {
            "survey_year": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey year."},
            "survey_quarter": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey quarter (1–4)."},
            "forecaster_id": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "SPF forecaster identifier."},
            "adjusted_cpi10": {"type": "float", "unit": "annualized percentage points", "desc": "Adjusted 10-year CPI expectation after removing realized CPI1 terms and re-averaging over the remaining quarters."},
        }
    },
    "inflation_news": {
        "file": "inflation_news.csv",
        "description": "Inflation news by survey and forecaster: current-quarter realized CPI1 minus the previous survey's CPI2 nowcast for that same realized quarter.",
        "primary_key": ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
        "foreign_keys": {},
        "variables": {
            "survey_year": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey year."},
            "survey_quarter": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey quarter (1-4)."},
            "forecaster_id": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "SPF forecaster identifier."},
            "inflation_news": {"type": "float", "unit": "annualized percentage points", "desc": "Current-quarter realized CPI1 minus the previous survey's CPI2 nowcast for that same quarter."},
        }
    },
    "reputation_measure": {
        "file": "reputation_measure.csv",
        "description": "Reputation measure rho constructed from long-term inflation expectation x and config-loaded structural parameters. The unit index is generic and may be a forecaster id or an aggregate identifier such as a summary type.",
        "primary_key": ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "u"],
        "foreign_keys": {},
        "variables": {
            "survey_year": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey year."},
            "survey_quarter": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey quarter (1-4)."},
            "u": {"type": "string or integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Generic unit index identifying the forecast unit, such as forecaster_id or summary_type."},
            "rho": {"type": "float", "unit": "-", "desc": "Reputation measure backed out from long-term inflation expectation x and config-loaded parameters."},
        }
    },
}


def load_table(filename):
    """Load a CSV table from the cleaned directory."""
    return pd.read_csv(CLEANED_DIR / filename)


def compute_summary(df):
    """Compute summary statistics for a dataframe."""
    summary = []
    for col in df.columns:
        stats = {
            "Variable": col,
            "N": len(df),
            "Missing": df[col].isna().sum(),
            "Unique": df[col].nunique(),
        }
        if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(df[col]):
            stats["Mean"] = f"{df[col].mean():.2f}"
            stats["Std"] = f"{df[col].std():.2f}"
            stats["Min"] = f"{df[col].min():.2f}"
            stats["Max"] = f"{df[col].max():.2f}"
        else:
            stats["Mean"] = "-"
            stats["Std"] = "-"
            stats["Min"] = "-"
            stats["Max"] = "-"
        summary.append(stats)
    return pd.DataFrame(summary)


def available_rows(df, value_col):
    """Keep rows with non-missing values in the target column."""
    out = df.copy()
    out[value_col] = pd.to_numeric(out[value_col], errors="coerce")
    return out.loc[out[value_col].notna()].copy()


def availability_summary(df, value_col):
    """Summarize the survey window for non-missing values."""
    available = available_rows(df=df, value_col=value_col)
    if len(available) == 0:
        return {
            "first_survey": "N/A",
            "last_survey": "N/A",
            "n_surveys_with_data": 0,
            "n_rows_with_data": 0,
        }
    survey_keys = available[["survey_year", "survey_quarter"]].drop_duplicates()
    first_row = available.sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"]).iloc[0]
    last_row = available.sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"]).iloc[-1]
    return {
        "first_survey": f"{int(first_row['survey_year'])}:Q{int(first_row['survey_quarter'])}",
        "last_survey": f"{int(last_row['survey_year'])}:Q{int(last_row['survey_quarter'])}",
        "n_surveys_with_data": int(len(survey_keys)),
        "n_rows_with_data": int(len(available)),
    }


def counts_by_survey(df, value_col, count_name):
    """Count forecasters with non-missing values by survey."""
    available = available_rows(df=df, value_col=value_col)
    if len(available) == 0:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["survey_year", "survey_quarter", count_name])
    return (
        available.groupby(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"], as_index=False)["forecaster_id"]
        .nunique()
        .rename(columns={"forecaster_id": count_name})
        .sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"])
    )


def previous_survey_keys(df):
    """Add previous-survey keys using Q1 -> previous year Q4 rollover."""
    out = df.copy()
    out["prev_survey_year"] = out["survey_year"]
    out["prev_survey_quarter"] = out["survey_quarter"] - 1
    q1_mask = out["survey_quarter"] == 1
    out.loc[q1_mask, "prev_survey_year"] = out.loc[q1_mask, "survey_year"] - 1
    out.loc[q1_mask, "prev_survey_quarter"] = 4
    return out


def revision_ready_counts(df, value_col, count_name):
    """Count forecasters with non-missing values in consecutive surveys."""
    available = available_rows(df=df, value_col=value_col)
    if len(available) == 0:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["survey_year", "survey_quarter", count_name])
    available = previous_survey_keys(
        available[["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"]].drop_duplicates()
    )
    prev = available[["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"]].rename(
        columns={
            "survey_year": "prev_survey_year",
            "survey_quarter": "prev_survey_quarter",
        }
    )
    matched = available.merge(
        prev,
        how="inner",
        on=["prev_survey_year", "prev_survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
    )
    return (
        matched.groupby(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"], as_index=False)["forecaster_id"]
        .nunique()
        .rename(columns={"forecaster_id": count_name})
        .sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"])
    )


def quarter_labels(df):
    """Format survey-year-quarter labels for plots."""
    return [f"{int(y)}:Q{int(q)}" for y, q in zip(df["survey_year"], df["survey_quarter"])]


def plot_summary_all(forecast_individual, adjusted):
    """Summarize raw and adjusted CPI10 by survey across all forecasters."""
    merged = forecast_individual[
        ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id", "CPI10"]
    ].merge(
        adjusted,
        how="inner",
        on=["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
    )
    merged["CPI10"] = pd.to_numeric(merged["CPI10"], errors="coerce")
    merged["adjusted_cpi10"] = pd.to_numeric(merged["adjusted_cpi10"], errors="coerce")
    merged = merged.loc[merged["CPI10"].notna() & merged["adjusted_cpi10"].notna()].copy()
    if len(merged) == 0:
        return pd.DataFrame(
            columns=[
                "survey_year",
                "survey_quarter",
                "mean_cpi10",
                "median_cpi10",
                "mean_adjusted_cpi10",
                "median_adjusted_cpi10",
            ]
        )
    return (
        merged.groupby(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"], as_index=False)
        .agg(
            mean_cpi10=("CPI10", "mean"),
            median_cpi10=("CPI10", "median"),
            mean_adjusted_cpi10=("adjusted_cpi10", "mean"),
            median_adjusted_cpi10=("adjusted_cpi10", "median"),
        )
        .sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"])
    )


def plot_summary_revision_ready(forecast_individual, adjusted):
    """Summarize raw and adjusted CPI10 for revision-ready forecasters only."""
    merged = forecast_individual[
        ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id", "CPI10"]
    ].merge(
        adjusted,
        how="inner",
        on=["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
    )
    merged["CPI10"] = pd.to_numeric(merged["CPI10"], errors="coerce")
    merged["adjusted_cpi10"] = pd.to_numeric(merged["adjusted_cpi10"], errors="coerce")
    merged = merged.loc[merged["CPI10"].notna() & merged["adjusted_cpi10"].notna()].copy()
    if len(merged) == 0:
        return pd.DataFrame(
            columns=[
                "survey_year",
                "survey_quarter",
                "mean_cpi10",
                "median_cpi10",
                "mean_adjusted_cpi10",
                "median_adjusted_cpi10",
            ]
        )
    available = previous_survey_keys(
        merged[["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"]].drop_duplicates()
    )
    prev = available[["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"]].rename(
        columns={
            "survey_year": "prev_survey_year",
            "survey_quarter": "prev_survey_quarter",
        }
    )
    revision_ready = available.merge(
        prev,
        how="inner",
        on=["prev_survey_year", "prev_survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
    )[["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"]]
    panel = merged.merge(
        revision_ready,
        how="inner",
        on=["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
    )
    return (
        panel.groupby(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"], as_index=False)
        .agg(
            mean_cpi10=("CPI10", "mean"),
            median_cpi10=("CPI10", "median"),
            mean_adjusted_cpi10=("adjusted_cpi10", "mean"),
            median_adjusted_cpi10=("adjusted_cpi10", "median"),
        )
        .sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"])
    )
```

## Table Overview

```{python}
#| echo: false
overview = []
for name, config in TABLES.items():
    filepath = CLEANED_DIR / config["file"]
    if filepath.exists():
        df = load_table(config["file"])
        rows = len(df)
    else:
        rows = "N/A"
    overview.append({
        "Table": f"`{config['file']}`",
        "Description": config["description"],
        "Primary Key": ", ".join(f"`{k}`" for k in config["primary_key"]),
        "Rows": rows,
    })
pd.DataFrame(overview)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

for name, config in TABLES.items():
    filepath = CLEANED_DIR / config["file"]
    
    # Section header
    print(f"\n## {config['file']}\n")
    print(f"{config['description']}\n")
    
    # Keys
    print("### Keys\n")
    print(f"- **Primary Key:** {', '.join(f'`{k}`' for k in config['primary_key'])}")
    if config["foreign_keys"]:
        print("- **Foreign Keys:**")
        for fk, ref in config["foreign_keys"].items():
            print(f"  - `{fk}` → `{ref}.csv`")
    else:
        print("- **Foreign Keys:** None")
    
    # Variables
    print("\n### Variables\n")
    if config["variables"]:
        print("| Variable | Type | Unit | Description |")
        print("|----------|------|------|-------------|")
        for var, meta in config["variables"].items():
            print(f"| `{var}` | {meta['type']} | {meta['unit']} | {meta['desc']} |")
    else:
        print("*TODO: Define variables in TABLES configuration*")
    
    # Summary statistics
    print("\n### Summary Statistics\n")
    if filepath.exists():
        df = load_table(config["file"])
        summary_df = compute_summary(df)
        print(summary_df.to_markdown(index=False))
    else:
        print("*Run the cleaning pipeline first to generate this table*")
    
    print("\n---\n")
```

## Forecast columns and forecaster counts

This section reports which forecast columns in `forecast_individual.csv` have at
least one non-missing value and the number of distinct forecasters by survey
(survey_year, survey_quarter).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

fi_path = CLEANED_DIR / "forecast_individual.csv"
if not fi_path.exists():
    print("*Run the cleaning pipeline to generate `forecast_individual.csv`.*")
else:
    fi = pd.read_csv(fi_path)
    id_cols = ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"]
    horizon_cols = [c for c in fi.columns if c not in id_cols]

    def is_missing(x):
        if pd.isna(x):
            return True
        if isinstance(x, str):
            s = x.strip().upper()
            return s == "#N/A" or s == "" or s == "NAN"
        return False

    available_horizons = [h for h in horizon_cols if (~fi[h].apply(is_missing)).any()]

    forecasters_by_survey = (
        fi.groupby(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"], as_index=False)["forecaster_id"]
        .nunique()
        .rename(columns={"forecaster_id": "n_forecasters"})
    )

    n_surveys = len(forecasters_by_survey)
    if n_surveys > 0:
        summary_df = pd.DataFrame(
            [
                {
                    "available_forecast_columns": ", ".join(available_horizons) if available_horizons else "—",
                    "n_surveys": n_surveys,
                    "min_forecasters": forecasters_by_survey["n_forecasters"].min(),
                    "mean_forecasters": f"{forecasters_by_survey['n_forecasters'].mean():.1f}",
                    "max_forecasters": forecasters_by_survey["n_forecasters"].max(),
                }
            ]
        )
    else:
        summary_df = pd.DataFrame(
            [
                {
                    "available_forecast_columns": ", ".join(available_horizons) if available_horizons else "—",
                    "n_surveys": 0,
                    "min_forecasters": "—",
                    "mean_forecasters": "—",
                    "max_forecasters": "—",
                }
            ]
        )
    print("\n### Summary\n")
    print(summary_df.to_markdown(index=False))

    print("\n### Forecaster counts by survey\n")
    print(forecasters_by_survey.to_markdown(index=False))
```

## Forecast Revision Regression Inputs

### Long-term inflation expectation

This section reports the individual-level long-term inflation expectation
constructed in `adjusted_cpi10.csv`. It summarizes the survey window with
non-missing adjusted CPI10, the number of forecasters by survey, and the
revision-ready subset with previous-quarter adjusted CPI10 available.

```{python long-term-inflation-codebook-table}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

adj_path = CLEANED_DIR / "adjusted_cpi10.csv"
if not adj_path.exists():
    print("*Run `scripts/adjust_spf_cpi10.py` to generate `adjusted_cpi10.csv`.*")
else:
    adjusted = pd.read_csv(adj_path)
    availability_df = pd.DataFrame([availability_summary(df=adjusted, value_col="adjusted_cpi10")])
    counts = counts_by_survey(
        df=adjusted,
        value_col="adjusted_cpi10",
        count_name="n_forecasters_with_adjusted_cpi10",
    )
    revision_ready = revision_ready_counts(
        df=adjusted,
        value_col="adjusted_cpi10",
        count_name="n_forecasters_with_prev_quarter_adjusted_cpi10",
    )
    merged = counts.merge(
        revision_ready,
        how="left",
        on=["survey_year", "survey_quarter"],
    )
    merged["n_forecasters_with_prev_quarter_adjusted_cpi10"] = (
        merged["n_forecasters_with_prev_quarter_adjusted_cpi10"].fillna(0).astype(int)
    )
    print("\n#### Availability Summary\n")
    print(availability_df.to_markdown(index=False))
    print("\n#### Forecasters by survey\n")
    print(merged.to_markdown(index=False))
```

```{python long-term-inflation-codebook-figure}
#| echo: false

fi_path = CLEANED_DIR / "forecast_individual.csv"
adj_path = CLEANED_DIR / "adjusted_cpi10.csv"
if not fi_path.exists() or not adj_path.exists():
    print("*Run the cleaning and CPI10 adjustment scripts to generate the required tables.*")
else:
    forecast_individual = pd.read_csv(fi_path)
    adjusted = pd.read_csv(adj_path)
    plot_all = plot_summary_all(
        forecast_individual=forecast_individual,
        adjusted=adjusted,
    )
    plot_revision_ready = plot_summary_revision_ready(
        forecast_individual=forecast_individual,
        adjusted=adjusted,
    )

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows=2, ncols=1, figsize=(10, 8), sharex=False)

    axes[0].plot(plot_all.index, plot_all["mean_cpi10"], label="Mean CPI10")
    axes[0].plot(plot_all.index, plot_all["median_cpi10"], label="Median CPI10")
    axes[0].plot(plot_all.index, plot_all["mean_adjusted_cpi10"], label="Mean adjusted CPI10")
    axes[0].plot(plot_all.index, plot_all["median_adjusted_cpi10"], label="Median adjusted CPI10")
    axes[0].set_title("CPI10 and adjusted CPI10: all forecasters")
    axes[0].set_ylabel("Annualized percentage points")
    if len(plot_all) > 0:
        tick_idx = list(range(0, len(plot_all), max(len(plot_all) // 8, 1)))
        axes[0].set_xticks(tick_idx)
        axes[0].set_xticklabels([quarter_labels(plot_all)[i] for i in tick_idx], rotation=45, ha="right")
    axes[0].legend()
    axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    axes[1].plot(plot_revision_ready.index, plot_revision_ready["mean_cpi10"], label="Mean CPI10")
    axes[1].plot(plot_revision_ready.index, plot_revision_ready["median_cpi10"], label="Median CPI10")
    axes[1].plot(plot_revision_ready.index, plot_revision_ready["mean_adjusted_cpi10"], label="Mean adjusted CPI10")
    axes[1].plot(plot_revision_ready.index, plot_revision_ready["median_adjusted_cpi10"], label="Median adjusted CPI10")
    axes[1].set_title("CPI10 and adjusted CPI10: revision-ready forecasters")
    axes[1].set_ylabel("Annualized percentage points")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Survey year-quarter")
    if len(plot_revision_ready) > 0:
        tick_idx = list(range(0, len(plot_revision_ready), max(len(plot_revision_ready) // 8, 1)))
        axes[1].set_xticks(tick_idx)
        axes[1].set_xticklabels(
            [quarter_labels(plot_revision_ready)[i] for i in tick_idx],
            rotation=45,
            ha="right",
        )
    axes[1].legend()
    axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

### Inflation news

This section reports the cleaned inflation-news table once it is generated. The
table should be keyed by `(survey_year, survey_quarter, forecaster_id)` and use
the timing rule `CPI1(y,q) - CPI2(y,q-1)` with the Q1 rollover to `(y-1,4)`.

```{python inflation-news-codebook-table}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

news_path = CLEANED_DIR / "inflation_news.csv"
if not news_path.exists():
    print("*Run the inflation-news construction step to generate `inflation_news.csv`.*")
else:
    inflation_news = pd.read_csv(news_path)
    availability_df = pd.DataFrame([availability_summary(df=inflation_news, value_col="inflation_news")])
    counts = counts_by_survey(
        df=inflation_news,
        value_col="inflation_news",
        count_name="n_forecasters_with_inflation_news",
    )
    revision_ready = revision_ready_counts(
        df=inflation_news,
        value_col="inflation_news",
        count_name="n_forecasters_with_prev_quarter_inflation_news",
    )
    merged = counts.merge(
        revision_ready,
        how="left",
        on=["survey_year", "survey_quarter"],
    )
    merged["n_forecasters_with_prev_quarter_inflation_news"] = (
        merged["n_forecasters_with_prev_quarter_inflation_news"].fillna(0).astype(int)
    )
    print("\n#### Availability Summary\n")
    print(availability_df.to_markdown(index=False))
    print("\n#### Forecasters by survey\n")
    print(merged.to_markdown(index=False))
```

```{python inflation-news-codebook-figure}
#| echo: false

news_path = CLEANED_DIR / "inflation_news.csv"
if not news_path.exists():
    print("*Run the inflation-news construction step to generate `inflation_news.csv`.*")
else:
    inflation_news = pd.read_csv(news_path)
    available = available_rows(df=inflation_news, value_col="inflation_news")
    if len(available) == 0:
        print("*`inflation_news.csv` exists but has no non-missing `inflation_news` values.*")
    else:
        survey_summary = (
            available.groupby(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"], as_index=False)
            .agg(
                mean_inflation_news=("inflation_news", "mean"),
                median_inflation_news=("inflation_news", "median"),
            )
            .sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"])
        )
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
        ax.plot(survey_summary.index, survey_summary["mean_inflation_news"], label="Mean inflation news")
        ax.plot(survey_summary.index, survey_summary["median_inflation_news"], label="Median inflation news")
        ax.set_title("Inflation news by survey")
        ax.set_ylabel("Annualized percentage points")
        ax.set_xlabel("Survey year-quarter")
        tick_idx = list(range(0, len(survey_summary), max(len(survey_summary) // 8, 1)))
        ax.set_xticks(tick_idx)
        ax.set_xticklabels([quarter_labels(survey_summary)[i] for i in tick_idx], rotation=45, ha="right")
        ax.legend()
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()
```

### Reputation measure

This section reports the cleaned reputation-measure table once it is generated.
The construction uses the long-term inflation expectation input `x` together
with config-loaded parameter values for `q`, `pi_target`, `pi_NE`, `z_a`, and
`z_alpha`.

```{python reputation-measure-codebook-table}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

rho_path = CLEANED_DIR / "reputation_measure.csv"
if not rho_path.exists():
    print("*Run the reputation-measure construction step to generate `reputation_measure.csv`.*")
else:
    reputation = pd.read_csv(rho_path)
    availability_df = pd.DataFrame([availability_summary(df=reputation, value_col="rho")])
    print("\n#### Availability Summary\n")
    print(availability_df.to_markdown(index=False))
    print("\n#### Summary Statistics\n")
    print(compute_summary(reputation).to_markdown(index=False))
```

```{python reputation-measure-codebook-figure}
#| echo: false

rho_path = CLEANED_DIR / "reputation_measure.csv"
if not rho_path.exists():
    print("*Run the reputation-measure construction step to generate `reputation_measure.csv`.*")
else:
    reputation = pd.read_csv(rho_path)
    available = available_rows(df=reputation, value_col="rho")
    if len(available) == 0:
        print("*`reputation_measure.csv` exists but has no non-missing `rho` values.*")
    else:
        survey_summary = (
            available.groupby(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"], as_index=False)
            .agg(
                mean_rho=("rho", "mean"),
                median_rho=("rho", "median"),
            )
            .sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"])
        )
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
        ax.plot(survey_summary.index, survey_summary["mean_rho"], label="Mean rho")
        ax.plot(survey_summary.index, survey_summary["median_rho"], label="Median rho")
        ax.set_title("Reputation measure by survey")
        ax.set_ylabel("rho")
        ax.set_xlabel("Survey year-quarter")
        tick_idx = list(range(0, len(survey_summary), max(len(survey_summary) // 8, 1)))
        ax.set_xticks(tick_idx)
        ax.set_xticklabels([quarter_labels(survey_summary)[i] for i in tick_idx], rotation=45, ha="right")
        ax.legend()
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()
```
